tool_args.label: LIST OF COMPETITIVE PROCUREMENTS FOR BOARD APPROVAL - June 2025

payload.title: LIST OF COMPETITIVE PROCUREMENTS FOR BOARD APPROVAL
payload.description: MTA Board meeting document listing competitive procurements requiring Board approval, including staff summaries for M-9A Passenger Railcars (Alstom), Hexagon EAM implementation (21Tech), FMTAC Portfolio Manager (BlackRock), and Energy Management System (Tango Analytics).

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metropolitan Transportation Authority
JUNE 2025
LIST OF COMPETITIVE PROCUREMENTS FOR BOARD APPROVAL

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Contract award for M-9A Passenger Railcars Procurement

evidence_refs.source_quote: Alstom Transportation Inc. $2,304,271,712 (estimated) Staff Summary Attached Term Contract #237848 Contract award for M-9A Passenger Railcars Procurement.
Long Island Rail Road ("LIRR") and Metro-North Commuter Railroad ("MNR"), collectively ("the Railroads")
Metro-North Commuter Railroad ("MNR"), collectively ("the Railroads")

payload.description: NYS Certified Minority-Owned Business Enterprise providing Hexagon EAM implementation consulting services

evidence_refs.source_quote: 21Tech LLC ("21Tech") to provide consulting services to replace a 29-year-old Computer Maintenance Management System
21Tech, a NYS Certified Minority-Owned Business Enterprise

payload.description: Portfolio Manager for FMTAC assets

evidence_refs.source_quote: BlackRock Financial Management Inc. ("BlackRock") to serve as the Portfolio Manager to provide portfolio management services in connection with the assets held within the First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company ("FMTAC")

payload.description: Energy Management System provider for MTA-wide utility billing data management

evidence_refs.source_quote: Tango Analytics LLC ("Tango"), to furnish and implement an MTA-wide Energy Management System ("EMS") for utility billing data management

payload.description: MTA's wholly owned captive insurance company

evidence_refs.source_quote: First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company ("FMTAC"), the MTA's wholly owned captive insurance company
MTA Construction & Development

payload.description: Design, manufacture, test, furnish, and delivery of 316 self-powered base order cars (160 LIRR/156 MNR) with options for 100 up to 242 additional cars. Contract award to Alstom Transportation Inc. Contract No. 237848.
payload.agency: Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Commuter Railroad

evidence_refs.source_quote: To obtain MTA Board approval authorizing Long Island Rail Road ("LIRR") and Metro-North Commuter Railroad ("MNR"), collectively ("the Railroads"), to award a purchase contract for M-9A Passenger Railcars to Alstom Transportation Inc. ("Alstom") in the estimated amount of $2,304,271,712, for the design, manufacture, test, furnish, and delivery of 316 (160 LIRR/156 MNR) self-powered base order cars.

tool_args.label: Hexagon EAM Implementation for NYCT Department of Buses and MTA Bus

payload.project_name: Hexagon System Implementation for NYCT Department of Bus and MTA Bus
payload.description: Replace 29-year-old Computer Maintenance Management System (CMMS) in 27 bus depots and four shops with new Hexagon Enterprise Asset Management System (EAM). Contract to 21Tech LLC. Contract No. 900000000005018.
payload.agency: MTA Department of Buses and MTA Bus Company

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Headquarters is seeking Board approval to award a competitively negotiated personal service contract to 21Tech LLC ("21Tech") to provide consulting services to replace a 29-year-old Computer Maintenance Management System ("CMMS") in 27 bus depots and four shops within the five boroughs to a new Hexagon Enterprise Asset Management System ("EAM") for NYC Transit's Department of Buses ("DOB") and MTA Bus Company ("MTABC").

("BlackRock") to serve as the Portfolio Manager to provide portfolio management services in connection with the assets held within the First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company ("FMTAC"), the MTA's wholly owned captive insurance company.

evidence_refs.source_quote: $1,499,780,544 for the option contract (242 cars)

evidence_refs.source_quote: delivery of 316 (160 LIRR/156 MNR) self-powered base order cars

payload.raw_value_text: $395 million or 14.6 percent less than Alstom's proposal
payload.description: Combined savings of $395 million or 14.6% less than Alstom's BAFO for base order cars

evidence_refs.source_quote: combined savings of $395 million or 14.6 percent less than Alstom's proposal for the base order cars

evidence_refs.source_quote: overall total $13,001,280

evidence_refs.source_quote: The value of the base four-year contract is $8,999,680

evidence_refs.source_quote: total not-to-exceed amount of $2,848,750

evidence_refs.source_quote: The estimated amount is $2,313,338 for up to a five-year period

evidence_refs.source_quote: based on an award made in July 2025

payload.description: Delivery of 316 M-9A cars scheduled to commence with pilot cars in Q3 2029

evidence_refs.source_quote: The delivery of the 316 cars, starting with the pilot cars, is scheduled to commence in the third quarter of 2029

payload.description: Delivery of 316 M-9A cars scheduled to conclude in Q4 2032

evidence_refs.source_quote: to conclude in the fourth quarter of 2032

payload.description: Start of base four-year contract term for Hexagon EAM implementation by 21Tech

evidence_refs.source_quote: July 2025–June 2029

payload.description: Start of initial three-year term for BlackRock portfolio management services for FMTAC

evidence_refs.source_quote: initial term of three years beginning August 1, 2025

payload.description: Base contract term end date for M-9A Passenger Railcars contract

evidence_refs.source_quote: Base: September 2036 / Option: March 2038

tool_args.label: M-9A cars needed to replace MNR M-3 and retire LIRR M-3 cars

payload.claim_text: The M-9A cars are needed to replace the MNR M-3 cars that are beyond their service life and retire the LIRR M-3 cars whose service has been extended to support Grand Central Madison service expansion.
payload.description: Purpose for M-9A procurement: replace aging M-3 railcars for both LIRR and MNR

evidence_refs.source_quote: The M-9A cars are needed to replace the MNR M-3 cars that are beyond their service life and retire the LIRR M-3 cars whose service has been extended to support Grand Central Madison ("GCM") service expansion.

tool_args.label: M-9A contract must follow FTA Procurement Guidelines

payload.claim_text: Because the LIRR cars were identified in the Federal Transit Administration (FTA)/MTA Full Funding Grant Agreement for GCM, the awarded M-9A contract is required to follow FTA Procurement Guidelines, including Buy America requirements.
payload.description: M-9A contract subject to FTA procurement rules and Buy America

evidence_refs.source_quote: Because the LIRR cars were identified in the Federal Transit Administration ("FTA")/MTA Full Funding Grant Agreement for GCM, the awarded M-9A contract is required to follow FTA Procurement Guidelines, including Buy America requirements.

tool_args.label: Alstom will comply with FTA Buy America requirement of 70 percent

payload.claim_text: Alstom will comply with the FTA Buy America requirement of 70 percent.
payload.description: Buy America compliance requirement for M-9A contract

evidence_refs.source_quote: Alstom will comply with the FTA Buy America requirement of 70 percent.

tool_args.label: Legacy CMMS is obsolete and no longer supported

payload.claim_text: Now obsolete, the CMMS can no longer be fully supported.
payload.description: Current Computer Maintenance Management System at DOB/MTABC is obsolete

evidence_refs.source_quote: Now obsolete, the CMMS can no longer be fully supported.

tool_args.label: Annual average bill auditing savings from EMS

payload.claim_text: Over the 2019–2024 time period, the annual average bill auditing savings were approximately $2.4 million.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Over the 2019–2024 time period, the annual average bill auditing savings were approximately $2.4 million.

payload.claim_text: Over the past five years, the MTA has spent more than $900 million annually on fuel and utilities with Agencies responsible for their own bill auditing.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Over the past five years, the MTA has spent more than $900 million annually on fuel and utilities
